summary: preinstall script exfiltrates host/user info to non-publisher domain
details: The package's dist/scripts/preinstall.js runs at npm install time and combines every signal of an installer-side data-exfiltration dropper: it imports fs/https/http/os, collects host information via os.userInfo() and os.networkInterfaces(), reads process.env, and issues https.get() to the hardcoded endpoint https://2tak.l.serverhost.name (a non-publisher, unrelated serverhost.name subdomain). The destination does not match the package scope/publisher (@gusmano) and is not a known runtime/SDK CDN, so there is no legitimate reason for a preinstall hook to contact it while simultaneously enumerating user account info, network interfaces, and environment variables. Filesystem checks (fs.existsSync at L32/L48) combined with the outbound request shape indicate host fingerprinting followed by beacon/exfiltration. Semantic tracing was incomplete, but the concrete lifecycle placement (preinstall.js), the concrete destination URL, and the concrete host-enumeration APIs are each individually sufficient installer-harm signals; jointly they constitute an install-time exfiltration beacon. Installers running `npm install @gusmano/reext` will execute this script without consent before any application code runs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
